title: solanum gilo var. pierreanum
intro: Solanum gilo var. pierreanum, also known as Pierre's wild eggplant, is a variant of the Solanum gilo plant, belonging to the nightshade family (Solanaceae) and is native to West Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Solanum gilo var. pierreanum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use in treating various digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it is also used in traditional cooking in some parts of West Africa.
